blurb: The hardiest peach and one of the last to bloom — the Front Range's best shot at homegrown peaches.
water: Moderate, regular — deep weekly soak in summer
seasons: Pink blossom mid-spring; red-blushed yellow freestone peaches Aug
toxic: Fruit flesh edible; pit, leaves & twigs cyanogenic — toxic to people/pets/livestock

shots.cap: Spring — pink peach blossom (shown on the species; 'Reliance' blooms the same, but later than most peaches) · © Annika Lindqvist (CC-BY) / iNaturalist
Summer — a Reliance peach ripening on the branch · © RevNaomi (CC BY-NC 2.0) / Flickr
Ripe fruit — the red-blushed, freestone Reliance peach with its foliage · © RevNaomi (CC BY-NC 2.0) / Flickr
